commit.message:
fix(tailscale): add repair workflow and fix bootstrap-prod skip_auth

- Add repair-tailscale-exitnode.yml: dedicated workflow to recover
  Tailscale exit nodes without a full blue/green reprovision.
  Supports dev/qa/prod; handles three cases:
    1. Running but exit node not advertised → re-run tailscale up
    2. tailscaled stopped → restart service, then re-advertise
    3. Full re-auth needed (NeedsLogin/NoState/force_reauth) →
       fetch fresh auth key from Vault, tailscale up --reset

- Fix bootstrap-prod-network-vm.yml: tailscale_skip_auth was
  hardcoded to true, making it impossible to force re-authentication
  via the workflow. Now a workflow_dispatch input (default: true to
  preserve existing behaviour).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  service can link the variable group. Access is controlled by Azure RBAC;
  IP-based restriction blocked ADO from its own service IPs (20.41.6.2),
  which are not in the AzureServices trusted-bypass list.

- providers.tf: switch azuredevops provider from SP credentials to PAT
  (var.ado_pat / ARGOCD_ADO_PAT). The Crossplane SP lacks 'Use' permission
  on the agent pool, which prevented azuredevops_agent_queue creation.
  The PAT has 'Agent Pools: Read & manage' scope.

- platform.tf: replace ubuntu:22.04 (Docker Hub, rate-limited) with
  mcr.microsoft.com/mirror/docker/library/ubuntu:22.04 so AKS nodes
  pull from MCR without Docker Hub rate limiting or access issues.

- platform.tf: increase kube-prometheus-stack Helm timeout 600→900s
  to accommodate the many components deploying on spot node pools.

commit.message:
network: fix genesis-confirmed never set on old-code/timeout paths, blocking fork resolution

verify_genesis_compatibility() returned true (compatible) on three fallback paths
(None hash, unexpected response type, timeout) but never called mark_genesis_confirmed().
Result: peers were perpetually non-genesis-confirmed and fork resolution was always
skipped with 'peer not genesis-confirmed (likely old code)', leaving nodes stuck on
minority forks indefinitely (observed: LW-London stuck 33 blocks behind).

Fix: call mark_genesis_confirmed() in all three 'assume compatible' arms so that
fork resolution can proceed once the 5-minute cooldown expires and the check reruns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: harden project memory with injection scanning, atomic writes, budget limits, and staleness tracking (#95)

- Add scanMemoryContent() to reject prompt-injection payloads, exfil patterns,
  and invisible Unicode in candidate/summary/architecture text
- Sanitize architecture snapshots on read, write, and bootstrap paths so no
  unsanitized text reaches the agent system prompt

  temp-file + rename with retry and fallback on Windows EPERM

  after a configurable number of later sessions

  low-confidence correction is not overridden by a higher-confidence
  superseded entry on reload

Glob-based dead-code detector with mutual-def and scheme-kind fixes

Rewrite find_dead_code.py to use .glob files produced by rocqc for
name-resolved, module-disambiguated dead-code detection.

Key improvements over the regex-based approach:
- DefKey = (full_lib_path, bare_name): A.f and B.f are distinct.
  Previously, a live B.f would mask a dead A.f (false negative).
- Transitive closure via BFS through a uses-graph built from R-lines.
- Correct handling of mutual Fixpoint with-clauses (R-lines carry
  fully-qualified names; :N suffix is stripped).

False-positive fixes added in this commit:

1. scheme-kind R-lines: Combined Scheme commands (e.g.
   'Combined Scheme typing_mutind from a, b, c') produce no def/prf
   glob record for the combined result.  The ingredient schemes (a, b,
   c) are referenced via R-lines with kind 'scheme'.  These are now
   treated as live roots instead of being attributed to an enclosing
   definition, so ingredient schemes are not falsely flagged as dead.

2. Mutual Lemma with-clauses: cross-references between co-proved lemmas
   ('Lemma A ... with B : ...') are absent from the glob entirely.
   A text scan for '^with <name> :' (colon, not :=) detects prf-kind
   companions and adds a uses edge primary→companion so that B is live
   whenever A is live.

3. Mutual Scheme/Fixpoint with-clauses: secondary Scheme ingredients
   generated in the same 'Scheme A := ... with B := ...' block are
   structurally inseparable from A.  Even when B is not included in a
   Combined Scheme, removing it from the Scheme block would weaken the
   generated induction principle.  A text scan for '^with <name> :='
   detects def-kind companions and adds the same primary→companion edge.

Also remove 5 dead definitions from LoCalSyntax.v that were correctly
identified by the improved detector:
- subst_lvar, subst_loc_in_laddr, subst_loc_in_locexp,
  subst_loc_in_val, subst_loc_in_bind
These exist in LoCalDynamic.v (live) but the LoCalSyntax.v copies are
unreferenced.  The glob-based detector correctly distinguishes the two
by fully-qualified library path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: derive cookie Secure flag from base URL, not NODE_ENV

When running in production over HTTP (e.g. LXC on local network),
NODE_ENV=production was setting the Secure cookie flag, causing the
browser to refuse sending the session cookie over plain HTTP. This
broke the OAuth state validation (state_mismatch error).

Now the Secure flag is set only when NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL starts
with https://, which correctly handles HTTP-in-production setups.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rework ballscrew joint model to match physical design

Replace old slider-crank (A/B pivot on thigh/shank) with the accurate
model: motor on top of upper-leg tube drives a nut up/down the shaft;
nut connects through a slot to the shank at distance R_ARM from the
knee pivot.

Key changes:
- leg_kinematics.py: replace ballscrew_length/velocity with
  nut_position(), nut_lever_arm(), nut_velocity(), nut_stroke_for_range()
  using virtual-work relationship F = tau / (R_ARM * |sin theta|)
- force_calculator.py: rename functions (nut_force_required,
  motor_torque_from_nut_force, motor_speed_rpm, motor_power);
  add CARRIAGE_DRAG_N=0 parameter; add motor RPM/power outputs
- main.py: update summary table with nut stroke, lever arm, screw params
- pybullet_sim.py: import CARRIAGE_DRAG_N

Results: peak nut force ~774 N, motor torque ~0.69 N*m (5mm lead, 90% eff)
Nut stroke: 92.7 mm over working range, 37.6 mm during gait cycle.

commit.message:
consensus: fix TOCTOU race in validate_block_rewards causing false reward-injection rejects

Step 2 was calling the live tier_for_wallet (fresh registry read) while Step 5
used the all_infos snapshot captured at the start of validation.  A masternode
that gossip-registered between those two reads would be classified as 'known'
in Step 2 (keeping unknown_non_producer_paid == 0, so Step 5 ran) but absent
from all_infos, causing the is_known_masternode fallback in Step 5 to return
false and hard-reject a fully valid block.

Fix: replace the live tier_for_wallet call with snapshot_tier_for_wallet, a
closure that searches the already-captured all_infos slice.  Steps 2 and 5 now
use the same registry snapshot.  If a wallet is absent from the snapshot,
Step 2 increments unknown_non_producer_paid, which causes Step 5's strict
bitmap address check to be skipped (the correct lenient path — pool amounts
are still fully validated in Steps 2-4).

This was causing the node to stall at height 1092, rejecting block 1093 from
all peers including whitelisted nodes, with spurious 'possible reward injection'
warnings for legitimate Silver-pool recipient TIME15E85jKLtMsWax7oQVTHy8toXHHVauxj8H.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Make Linux wipe script safer for network filesystems (#41812)

This pull request enhances the safety of the `linux_wipe.sh` script by
ensuring that destructive file operations do not affect network-mounted
filesystems. The changes introduce checks to detect network filesystems,
prevent accidental deletion of remote data, and improve the reliability
of wipe operations by avoiding crossing filesystem boundaries.

**Network filesystem safety improvements:**

* Added a `NETWORK_FS_TYPES` variable and functions to detect and
unmount network filesystems, preventing the script from deleting data on
NFS, CIFS, SMB, SSHFS, and similar mounts.
(`ee/server/service/embedded_scripts/linux_wipe.sh`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7ac85220cbd45e63481837a405dacf198822a4fbf885b88f89b9bc870c947fccR3-R4)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-7ac85220cbd45e63481837a405dacf198822a4fbf885b88f89b9bc870c947fccR17-R84)
* Introduced an `unmount_network_filesystems` function called before
wiping operations to unmount all detected network filesystems.
(`ee/server/service/embedded_scripts/linux_wipe.sh`)
* Added an `is_network_mount` function to skip wiping any path residing
on a network filesystem.
(`ee/server/service/embedded_scripts/linux_wipe.sh`)

**Safe file deletion enhancements:**

* Implemented a `safe_rm` function that ensures file deletions do not
cross filesystem boundaries, using `rm --one-file-system` or `find
-xdev` as a fallback. All destructive operations now use this wrapper.
(`ee/server/service/embedded_scripts/linux_wipe.sh`)
* Updated `wipe_non_essential_data` and `wipe_system_files` to use
`safe_rm` and to skip paths on network filesystems.
(`ee/server/service/embedded_scripts/linux_wipe.sh`)

These changes significantly reduce the risk of deleting data on remote
or shared filesystems during a wipe operation.
